question: Write a short paragraph (4-6 sentences) describing a rainy day from the perspective of a child.
Write a catchy two-sentence tagline for a new AI evaluation tool that helps users pick the best AI model for their needs.
Write a short analogy explaining how a neural network works to a 10-year-old.

criteria: uses accessible language for a child
does not use jargon without explanation

question: Write a short tweet (max 280 characters) promoting the importance of AI safety.

criteria: uses appropriate tone for social media
includes a call to action or thought-provoking message

question: Describe the concept of recursion using a real-world, non-technical metaphor.
Write three unique names for a fictional AI assistant aimed at helping students with homework.

criteria: names are distinct from each other
at least one is creative or memorable

question: Write a brief (3-4 sentence) science fiction scenario where an AI model evaluation tool saves the day.

criteria: evaluation tool is central to the plot

question: Write a short explanation of what a large language model is for someone who has never heard of AI.
Write a short persuasive paragraph arguing that benchmarks alone are insufficient for evaluating AI models.

criteria: gives at least two specific reasons

question: Describe in 2-3 sentences how you would explain the difference between supervised and unsupervised learning using cooking as a metaphor.
Write a headline and one-sentence summary for a news story about a new AI model achieving human-level performance on a medical exam.
Write a short dialogue (4-6 lines) between a sceptical user and an AI evaluation tool discussing whether the AI's score can be trusted.
Write a short abstract (100-150 words) for a fictional research paper proposing a new user-centred approach to AI model evaluation.
Write a simple step-by-step guide (3-4 steps) explaining how someone with no technical background could use an AI evaluation tool.

criteria: steps are in a logical order

question: Create a brief (2-3 sentence) elevator pitch for this AI evaluation project to a potential investor.
Write a short comparison (4-6 sentences) of two different approaches to AI evaluation: benchmark testing vs user-scenario testing. Use an accessible analogy.

criteria: identifies at least one strength per approach
identifies at least one weakness per approach

question: Suggest three possible names for a website that hosts community-contributed AI model evaluation results.

criteria: names are relevant to the purpose

question: Write a 3-4 sentence introduction for a YouTube video reviewing and comparing three AI models.

criteria: clearly sets up the video's purpose
mentions the three models will be compared

question: Write a short critical commentary (5-6 sentences) on the limitations of using leaderboards to rank AI models.

criteria: identifies at least two concrete limitations
references the concept of benchmark gaming or overfitting

question: Write a short paragraph explaining why it matters which AI model a user chooses for a given task.

criteria: explains that different models have different strengths
explains potential consequences of a wrong choice

question: Write a short paragraph (3-4 sentences) to describe what this AI evaluation project does, suitable for a project README file on GitHub.
Explain the concept of 'hallucination' in large language models to a non-technical audience using a memorable analogy.

criteria: correctly conveys that the model generates false content confidently
does not oversimplify to the point of inaccuracy

question: Write a short (4-5 sentence) discussion on why ethical reasoning is particularly difficult to evaluate in AI models.

criteria: identifies subjectivity as a key challenge
considers the risk of encoding biases

question: Write a brief disclaimer (2-3 sentences) that could appear at the top of an AI model evaluation report, informing readers of the tool's limitations.
Write a 3-4 sentence description of what a radar chart (spider chart) shows, suitable for a report explaining evaluation results to a non-technical reader.

criteria: accurately describes what a radar chart shows

question: Write a short (5-6 sentence) argument for why community-contributed evaluation data is more valuable than single-user testing for AI model comparison.

criteria: identifies diversity of use cases as a benefit
discusses statistical robustness with more data

question: Write a brief (2-3 sentence) instruction telling a user how to add their own questions to the evaluation dataset.
Create a short (3-4 sentence) explanation of what API rate limiting is, suitable for including in a user guide for a non-technical user.

criteria: gives a real-world analogy or example
explains why it matters to the user

question: Write 2-3 sentences explaining why temperature=0 is used when querying AI models for evaluation purposes.
Write a short (5-6 sentence) conclusion section for a report evaluating three AI models, noting that no single model excels at everything.

criteria: summarises the key finding that no model dominates
